channel.title: Cyber Security & Linux | Murat Kurkoglu - orioninsist
channel.description: Recent content on Cyber Security & Linux | Murat Kurkoglu - orioninsist

item.title: Fix Broken Symlink Cleanup in Large Linux Deployments
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn safe broken symlink cleanup in Linux using a minimal Bash script to detect and remove stale links across mounted directories in Docker.

item.title: Fix Silent File Corruption in Bash Log Processing
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Detect silently truncated log files in Bash batch pipelines and isolate corrupted inputs before ingestion breaks analytics or downstream automation.

item.title: Detecting Stale NFS Mounts That Freeze CLI Operations
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how to detect stale NFS mounts that make ls, df, and shell commands hang using a minimal Bash timeout scan and reproducible test.

item.title: Kill Process Port Linux
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how to find and kill a Linux process using a specific port with lsof and kill. Includes a quick command and Bash automation.

item.title: Pacman Install Upgrade History
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how to check Pacman install, upgrade, and removal history in Arch Linux using pacman.log to audit package changes and troubleshoot issues.

item.title: System Status Snapshot Cpu Ram Disk Ports
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: One-command system status snapshot: load/CPU, RAM, disk usage, listening ports, and top processes—run in a minimal Docker lab for repeatable results.

item.title: My Cybersecurity Career: The Journey to SOC Tier 1 Begins
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I am documenting my step-by-step process, technical notes, and lessons learned while training to become a Junior Security Analyst on TryHackMe.

item.title: Linux logrotate: Prevent Disk Full Outages Without Losing Logs
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how Linux logrotate prevents disk full incidents while preserving useful logs for security and troubleshooting, with safe configs and examples.

item.title: 7 Bash Commands That Saved My Career (And Sanity)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Stop using the mouse. Discover the 7 Linux commands that transform you from a beginner to an undisputed expert.

item.title: 5 Bash Commands That Separate Junior Devs from Senior Engineers
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Stop clicking. Learn rsync, lsof, and grep to move from 'user' to 'admin'. The GUI is holding you back.

item.title: Stop Typing Full Paths (You Are Wasting Hours)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Absolute vs. Relative paths explained. Why using 'cd -' and '..' makes you a faster, better engineer.

item.title: Why I Threw Away My Mouse (The Shell vs. GUI Debate)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Stop clicking. Start commanding. Why the Linux terminal is faster, stronger, and necessary for modern engineering.

item.title: The Black Screen: I Formatted Everything and Now I'm Lost
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I nuked my GUI. No mouse, no icons, just a blinking cursor. Here is how I survived the first 5 minutes using whoami and pwd.

item.title: Creating a Professional Favicon Pipeline on Arch Linux
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: How I built a clean, reliable favicon workflow using ImageMagick, OptiPNG, and Hugo PaperMod on Arch Linux.

item.title: The Notes That Finally Made OpenAI Click for Me
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: How summarizing OpenAI into ultra-short notes helped me understand its models, mission, and ecosystem clearly for the first time.

item.title: Building a Cyber Security Home Lab: Why Local Hardware Beats the Cloud
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Cloud labs are convenient, but local virtualization offers control. Here is why I switched to a local KVM setup on Arch Linux for my Master's studies.

item.title: Why My Home Lab Became the Safest Place I Work
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: How I built a reliable, isolated, and predictable cyber security lab using virtualization and simple command-line controls.

item.title: Automating Linux Backups with rsync: A Set-and-Forget Strategy
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how to secure your data using rsync and cron. A complete guide to incremental backups for Linux systems.

item.title: Essential Wayland Tools for a Fast and Stable Sway Setup (2025 Developer Edition)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A complete guide to the essential Wayland-native tools that make Sway fast, stable, and productivity-ready for developers. Includes tool list, configurations, and workflow optimization tips.

item.title: The Ultimate Sway Config for a Fast and Stable Wayland Setup (2025 Edition)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A complete, production-grade Sway configuration optimized for performance, stability, and minimalism on Wayland. Includes best practices, recommended packages, environment variables, and a full ready-to-use config.

item.title: Optimize Sway on Wayland for Maximum Performance on Intel iGPU (Dual Full HD Setup)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A complete, high-precision performance optimization guide for Sway on Wayland using Intel integrated graphics, dual Full HD monitors, Mesa tuning, tear-free rendering, kernel parameters, and advanced compositor tweaks.

item.title: chmod vs chown: The Practical Differences Explained
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn the real differences between chmod and chown, how each affects Linux file permissions, and the exact scenarios where they fix permission errors.

item.title: How to Extract Multi-Part ZIP Files on Linux (The Correct Way)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how to correctly repair, merge, and extract multi-part ZIP files on Linux using zip -FF, with step-by-step commands for Arch Linux, Ubuntu, and Debian. Fix filename not matched, EOF, and other unzip errors fast.

item.title: Minimalism: Achieve Financial Freedom and Hyper-Focus by Decluttering Your Life
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A comprehensive guide on applying minimalist principles to finances, time, and physical space for maximum personal growth and sustainable happiness.

item.title: My Digital Attic: Why I Deleted 50,000 Photos
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I deleted 50,000 photos from my digital attic. It wasn't about storage, but the emotional cost of digital hoarding. A story of digital minimalism.

item.title: Bash 'cd' Command: Master Terminal Navigation Now
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Stop feeling lost in the Linux terminal. Master the 'cd' command, relative vs. absolute paths, and the back button trick 'cd -' to navigate like a pro.

item.title: Bash 'ls': How -lah Unlocks Your File System
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Ever felt lost in your terminal? Master the 'ls' command and its essential flags (-l, -a, -h) to finally 'see' your file system clearly.

item.title: Bash 'pwd': Your 'You Are Here' Map in the Terminal
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Ever feel lost in the Linux terminal? Learn why 'pwd' (Print Working Directory) is more than a command—it's the anchor for your mental model.

item.title: Bash echo: The Deep Dive on Single vs. Double Quotes
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn more than just printing. This 1500-word guide explores the Bash echo command, detailing the crucial difference between single and double quotes.

item.title: Mastering Bash echo: Quotes & Concatenation
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn more than just printing. This guide explores the Bash echo command, detailing the crucial difference between single and double quotes for mastering strings.

item.title: Terminal vs. Shell vs. Bash: Clearing Up the Confusion
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Confused by Terminal, Shell, and Bash? This guide clarifies the essential differences, explains the CLI, and shows why Bash became the standard.

item.title: Optimized Foot Config for Sway/Wayland Devs
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: My complete, optimized foot.ini config for Sway/Wayland. Learn how to get the perfect minimalist terminal with JetBrains Mono, Dracula, and CSD disabled.

item.title: Arch Hibernate: My Empty /boot/loader/entries/ Fix
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Couldn't enable hibernate on Arch Linux with systemd-boot? My /boot/loader/entries/ was empty. Here’s the fix: Unified Kernel Images (UKI) and /etc/kernel/cmdline.

item.title: Wayland Broke My Scripts: Meet wtype, Your New xdotool
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Frustrated that xdotool is broken in Wayland? Learn how to use wtype, the modern tool for keyboard and mouse automation in Sway and Hyprland.

item.title: Rofi vs. Wofi vs. Wmenu: The Ultimate Wayland & X11 Guide
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Rofi vs. Wofi vs. Wmenu: Which app launcher is best for your X11 or Wayland setup? A deep-dive comparison of features, configuration, and philosophy.

item.title: Markdown Fences: ``` vs. ~~~ — Which Do You Use?
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Ever wondered about ``` vs. ~~~ in Markdown? Discover the difference, which is standard, and the 'pro trick' for nesting code blocks you need to know.

item.title: My 4K Video Automation Script with FFmpeg & Zsh
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn to automate your video workflow. This guide shows how to build a Bash script with FFmpeg to merge, 4K-upscale, and watermark vertical videos.

item.title: My Arch + Sway Workflow: Blazing-Fast PWA Keybindings
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A deep dive into my Arch Linux + Sway keybindings. Learn my Mod4 strategy and how I use a custom shell script to launch web apps like ChatGPT as PWAs.

item.title: Fix Sway's Flaky Keyboard Layout Switching for Good
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Fix flaky Sway keyboard layout switching for good. This guide details a robust fix using Mod4+space and `swaymsg input type:keyboard` for `xkb_layout`.

item.title: What Is the ~/go Folder & Is It Safe to Delete?
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Discovered a mysterious 'go' directory in your Linux home folder? This guide explains what the Go module cache is, why tools like Hugo create it, and how to safely clean it with go clean -modcache to reclaim disk space.

item.title: Stop Linux Auto-Creating a ~/Downloads Folder
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Tired of the Downloads folder reappearing on your minimalist Arch Linux + Sway setup? This guide shows how to edit the XDG user-dirs.dirs file to permanently set your home directory as the default.

item.title: A Modern GNU Screen Setup on Arch Linux
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Supercharge your terminal workflow on Arch Linux! A deep dive into installing GNU Screen and using a custom .screenrc to get modern features like tmux.

item.title: The Sway Samurai: Mastering the Terminal with Zellij on Arch Linux
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Deep dive into adopting Zellij, the modern terminal multiplexer, on an Arch Linux, Sway Wayland setup. I detail the immediate productivity gains using its default configuration.

item.title: Fix Sway Volume Keys & Binding Warnings for Good
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A definitive guide to configuring volume keys in the Sway window manager on Arch Linux. Learn to use pamixer and fix the 'Overwriting binding' warning for good.

item.title: My Fix for Clean Hugo URLs with Dated Folders
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Tired of messy, date-filled URLs in your Hugo site? Learn the one-line fix in hugo.toml to organize content in dated folders while keeping clean, flat permalinks.

item.title: My Simple Pacman.conf for a Better Arch Experience
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Tired of a bland terminal? Learn how to transform your Arch Linux pacman experience with two simple edits in pacman.conf for a colorful, readable, and faster setup.

item.title: Supercharge Your Sway Workflow: Instant AI Hotkeys
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how to create instant keyboard shortcuts in the Sway Wayland compositor for AI tools like ChatGPT and Gemini, maximizing productivity on Arch Linux.

item.title: A100 Power, Browser Simplicity: Ollama on Colab Web UI
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I show you the fastest way to test LLMs like Llama 3 and Qwen. Use Ollama on Google Colab Pro’s A100 GPU and connect via a simple web UI—no local setup or coding required.

item.title: AI-Driven Sway Configs: Code Llama Solved My Wayland Headaches
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I detail my struggle with complex Wayland/Sway config files and how I used Code Llama and Ollama to automate, debug, and generate my perfect Linux desktop setup. A must-read for DevOps and advanced Linux users.

item.title: Arch/Sway: Manually Hacking the GTK File Chooser for Ultimate Workflow Speed
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn to manually configure the GTK File Chooser Dialog's bookmarks and settings in Arch Linux and Sway. Ditch the visual tools for ultimate workflow efficiency.

item.title: GUI-less Screen Capture: The wf-recorder/slurp Guide for Sway
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Dive into the terminal-only method for perfect screen recording on Arch Linux and Sway/Wayland. Learn to use wf-recorder and slurp for selected-area video capture.

item.title: Mastering Terminal GIF Screencasts on Arch Linux Sway
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I mastered creating high-quality, lightweight GIFs from screen recordings on my Arch Linux Sway/Wayland setup, using terminal tools `wf-recorder` and optimized `ffmpeg` commands.

item.title: Ditch the GUI: Mastering PipeWire Audio in Arch CLI
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I detail my full transition from Pavucontrol to CLI-only audio management on Arch Linux with Sway/PipeWire, using WirePlumber rules and wpctl for a minimal workflow.

item.title: FEH to SWAYIMG: Mastering Visual Workflow on Sway/Wayland
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: My deep dive into moving from X11's FEH to Wayland-native SWAYIMG on Arch Linux. Learn the Grim + Slurp terminal method for clean, fast screen capture.

item.title: Fedora Sway Spin vs. Arch: The True Cost of Wayland Control
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I compared the ready-to-use Fedora Sway Spin with my custom Arch Sway setup. Discover the critical differences in stability, performance, and the cost of total control.

item.title: Taming The Glare: Force Dark Mode On All Websites In Chrome
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn the secret Chrome flag, "Auto Dark Mode for Web Contents," to force a dark theme on any website, including platforms like Google Analytics, reducing eye strain.

item.title: Achieving Seamless, System-Wide Dark Mode on Arch Linux with Sway (Wayland): The Definitive Terminal Guide
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A deep, terminal-focused guide on fixing persistent dark mode conflicts in Arch Linux/Sway (Wayland). Learn to unify GTK 2, 3, 4, and Firefox/LibreWolf themes perfectly.

item.title: Neovim: More Than a Code Editor, It's a Mindset
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Discover why Neovim is more than a code editor. A personal journey into its philosophy of extensibility, control, and how it can transform your workflow forever.

item.title: My First 30 Days as a SOC Analyst: Lessons Learned
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: My first 30 days as a SOC Analyst: the real challenges, lessons learned, and tips I wish I knew starting out in cybersecurity.

item.title: My Google Colab Data Kept Vanishing. Here's How I Fixed It.
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Stop losing hours of work in Google Colab. Learn a robust workflow to manage large datasets and assets efficiently, overcoming slow speeds and session timeouts.

item.title: How I Built My Content-Producing Prompt Engine
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A detailed, first-person account of how I designed and built a powerful, single-prompt content engine to automate high-quality blog post creation.

item.title: My Hybrid AI Workflow: The Workshop and The Library
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Struggling to balance messy notebooks and clean code? I share my hybrid "Workshop and Library" model, blending Google Colab for research and a local IDE for production.

item.title: My Jellyfin Setup in a Debian LXC Container
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how to install Jellyfin Media Server in a Debian LXC container on Proxmox. This step-by-step guide covers setup, storage, and troubleshooting.

item.title: The Architect's Choice: Proxmox KVM vs. LXC Deep Dive
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Stuck between Proxmox KVM vs LXC? This in-depth guide explains the key differences in performance, security, and use cases to help you choose wisely.

item.title: Build an Enterprise-Grade Cloud at Home: The Ultimate Proxmox Setup Guide (Part 1)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Ready to build a powerful home server? Our A-Z guide walks you through Proxmox VE installation, from hardware selection to your first login. Start now!

item.title: The Ultimate Backup Constitution with rclone
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: My definitive guide to preventing data loss. This 'Backup Constitution' uses rclone to establish a robust, encrypted, and automated backup system on Linux.

item.title: Digital Chaos to Zen Clarity: How I Rebuilt My Life with the P.A.R.A. Method
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Overwhelmed by digital clutter? Discover how I used the P.A.R.A. method on Arch Linux to build a 'Second Brain' and achieve zen-like organization.

item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Journey from a bloated desktop user to the architect of a minimalist Arch Linux and Sway system. Discover the philosophy of taking back control.

item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: No degree? No problem. Learn how to start a cybersecurity career through self-learning, certifications, and hands-on skills. A roadmap for beginners.

item.title: Your First Step in Cybersecurity: SOC and Certifications
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: If you're wondering how to get into the cybersecurity world, this article is for you. Discover the most common entry-level positions, essential skills, and crucial certifications.

item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: The Orioninsist.org Privacy Policy provides detailed information about what data we collect, how we use it, and your privacy rights while using our site.

item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn about the most common types of cyber attacks. This beginner's guide explains what Phishing, Malware, and DDoS attacks are and how to protect yourself from them.

item.title: Cybersecurity Basics Cia Triad
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: This article explains the core principles of the CIA Triad—Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability—and their importance in information security. A perfect guide for beginners.

item.title: Why Networking Fundamentals Are the Foundation of a Soc Analyst Career
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A comprehensive guide for beginners on why core networking concepts like TCP/IP, DNS, and routing are the essential bedrock for any aspiring SOC analyst to detect and respond to cyber threats effectively.

item.description: Data science projects follow structured steps from problem definition to model monitoring for business impact.

item.description: Explore the fundamentals of data science, including processes, objectives, and its value in driving business innovation.

item.title: Mastering Variables and Syntax in Programming
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Syntax: In programming languages, syntax refers to the set of rules that defines the combinations of symbols that are considered to be correctly structured programs. It essentially dictates how code must be written. High-Level Languages: These are programming languages that are closer to human languages, making them easier to read and write. An example is Python. Low-Level Languages: These languages are closer to machine language, making them less readable for humans. Examples include C and C++.

item.title: Understanding Compilers Interpreters
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description:
Compilers and Interpreters

When programming, we use compilers or interpreters to convert our code into machine language that a computer can understand.

Compiler

A compiler is a software tool that translates source code written in a programming language into machine code. This process is called “compiling.” The output is usually an executable file that can be run on a specific processor architecture.

Process: Source Program –> Compiler –> Target Program (Machine Language)

item.title: Computers Python Basics
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn the basics of computers and Python, covering components, programs, programming languages, and how machine language uses binary code (0s and 1s) to process information.

item.title: Introduction to Algorithms
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Python is one of the most beginner-friendly programming languages, known for its simplicity and versatility

item.title: A Guide to Staying Safe in the Digital World
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Navigate the ever-changing landscape of cybersecurity and information security with this comprehensive guide. Learn essential strategies to protect your data and safeguard your digital assets from evolving cyber threats.

item.title: Docker Cli Essential Commands
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Explore Docker CLI with essential commands for listing, running, inspecting, and managing containers.

item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Unleash the power of Docker! This guide equips you with the fundamental commands to manage and navigate the world of containerized applications. Learn how to install, run, manage, and explore containers, all within a user-friendly format.

item.title: Exploring Docker Commands and Container Management
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Explore Docker commands and container management with this comprehensive guide. Learn how to update container images, create and manage files within containers, and monitor and interact with running containers.

item.description: Explore OrionInsist's Cutting-Edge Insights for November 2023

item.title: How to Install Docker Desktop on Debian
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description:
Docker is a powerful platform that allows you to develop, ship, and run applications inside containers. Installing Docker on Debian is a straightforward process, and in this guide, we’ll walk you through the steps to get Docker Desktop up and running on your Debian-based system.

  • Prerequisites Before we begin, make sure you have the following prerequisites in place:

A Debian-based system (such as Debian itself or Ubuntu) Administrative (sudo) privileges

item.title: Understanding Docker Containers
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Docker containers: Learn core concepts, benefits, and use cases in this detailed exploration. Streamline your software development and deployment with Docker.

item.description: Docker simplifying devOps with portable containers

item.description: Master Linux commands, shortcuts, and permissions for effective Linux usage

item.description: Explore vital Linux commands like 'cd,' 'ls,' 'file,' and more, plus valuable tips and shortcuts. Perfect for beginners mastering Linux commands.

item.title: Mastering Essential Linux Commands

item.description: Learn Linux essentials: cp, mv, clear, and more. Boost your productivity with these vital Linux commands.

item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn essential Linux commands: ls, grep, cd, man, and more in this comprehensive guide

item.title: Essential Linux Terminal Commands and Examples
